channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Selective excitation of work-generating cycles in non-reciprocal living solids
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 03 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03178-7

Starfish embryos can form living chiral crystals. Now it is shown that these crystals can spontaneously, as well as inducibly, transition between two stable states: fluctuating and oscillatory.
item.dc:title: Selective excitation of work-generating cycles in non-reciprocal living solids
item.dc:creator: Yu-Chen Chao
Shreyas Gokhale
Lisa Lin
Alasdair Hastewell
Alexandru Bacanu
Yuchao Chen
Junang Li
Jinghui Liu
Hyunseok Lee
Jörn Dunkel
Nikta Fakhri

item.title: Co-propagating photonic topological interface states with hybridized pseudo-spins
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 02 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03172-z

Topological interface states typically support the propagation of a single state in each direction, which limits their applicability. Now, co-propagating states are achieved in a photonic topological insulator system.
item.dc:title: Co-propagating photonic topological interface states with hybridized pseudo-spins
item.dc:creator: Xilin Feng
Tianwei Wu
Li Ge
Liang Feng

item.title: Towards advanced polarized electron sources
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 02 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03138-7

Gallium arsenide photocathodes inside a superconducting radio-frequency gun are a promising source of polarized electrons for future colliders. Now the operation of such a source has been demonstrated.
item.dc:title: Towards advanced polarized electron sources
item.dc:creator: Vladimir N. Litvinenko
Nikhil Bachhawat
Jean Clifford Brutus
Luca Cultrera
Kenneth Decker
Mengjia Gaowei
Patrick Inacker
Yichao Jing

Kali Prasanna Mondal
Geetha Narayan
Igor Pinayev
Freddy Severino
Kai Shih
John Skaritka
Loralie Smart
Yatming Than
John Walsh
Erdong Wang
Gang Wang
Dan Weiss

item.title: Observation of dissipationless fractional Chern insulator
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 30 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03167-2

Fractional Chern insulators have been observed in moiré MoTe2 at zero magnetic field, but the expected zero longitudinal resistance has not been demonstrated. Now it is shown that improving device quality allows this effect to appear.
item.dc:title: Observation of dissipationless fractional Chern insulator
item.dc:creator: Heonjoon Park
Weijie Li
Chaowei Hu
Christiano Beach
Miguel Gonçalves
Juan Felipe Mendez-Valderrama
Jonah Herzog-Arbeitman
Takashi Taniguchi
Kenji Watanabe
David Cobden
Liang Fu
B. Andrei Bernevig
Nicolas Regnault
Jiun-Haw Chu
Di Xiao
Xiaodong Xu

item.title: Lattice surgery realized on two distance-three repetition codes with superconducting qubits
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 30 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03090-6

Quantum error correction codes protect quantum information, but running algorithms also requires the ability to perform gates on logical qubits. A lattice surgery scheme for fault-tolerant gates has now been demonstrated in a quantum repetition code.
item.dc:title: Lattice surgery realized on two distance-three repetition codes with superconducting qubits
item.dc:creator: Ilya Besedin
Michael Kerschbaum
Jonathan Knoll
Ian Hesner
Lukas Bödeker
Luis Colmenarez
Luca Hofele

Dante Colao Zanuz
